testcase.name: test_stream_response_success
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.llms.test_cohere_handler.TestCohereHandlerStreaming testMethod=test_stream_response_success> mock_api_key = <MagicMock name='api_key' id='4378628816'> mock_perform_stream_request = <MagicMock name='perform_stream_request' id='4378761936'>
@patch.object(CohereClient, "perform_stream_request") @patch("tinychat.llms.base.BaseLLMClient.api_key", new_callable=MagicMock) def test_stream_response_success(self, mock_api_key, mock_perform_stream_request): # Setting a dummy value for mock_api_key is not strictly needed here
# Mocking the stream of responses mock_stream = [b'{"event_type": "text-generation", "text": "Hi!"}', b'{"event_type": "text-generation", "text": "How can I help?"}'] mock_perform_stream_request.return_value = mock_stream
handler = CohereHandler() user_input = "hello" generator = handler.stream_response(user_input)
# Collecting responses from the generator > responses = [resp for resp in generator] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/llms/test_cohere_handler.py:23: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ tests/llms/test_cohere_handler.py:23: in <listcomp> responses = [resp for resp in generator] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <tinychat.llms.cohere.CohereHandler object at 0x104fcb510> user_input = 'hello'
def stream_response(self, user_input: str) -> Generator[str, None, None]: """ Yield stream responses from the client as they are received.
This method sends the user input to the client and then yields each piece of the language model's response as it is received in real-time. After the streaming is complete, it updates the message list with the user input and the full language model response.
:param user_input: The input string from the user to be sent to the model. :return: A generator yielding the model's response in streamed parts. """ self._chat_history.append({"role": "User", "message": user_input}) stream = self._client.perform_stream_request(user_input, self._chat_history) lm_response = "" > for response_piece in stream.iter_text(): # type: ignore ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'iter_text'
tinychat/llms/cohere.py:84: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_perform_stream_request_failure

failure.message: TypeError: 'SSEClient' object is not an iterator

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.llms.test_google_client.TestGoogleAIClientStreaming testMethod=test_perform_stream_request_failure> mock_api_key = <MagicMock name='api_key' id='4379054800'> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4378720528'>
@patch("tinychat.llms.google.httpx.post") @patch("tinychat.llms.base.BaseLLMClient.api_key", new_callable=MagicMock) def test_perform_stream_request_failure(self, mock_api_key, mock_post): # Setting a dummy value for mock_api_key is not strictly needed here
# Mocking the response with an error status code mock_response = Mock(spec=Response) mock_response.status_code = 400 mock_post.return_value = mock_response
client = GoogleAIClient(temperature = 0.0) messages = [{"parts": [{"text": "content"}], "role": "user"}]
with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as context: > next(client.perform_stream_request(messages)) # type: ignore ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: 'SSEClient' object is not an iterator
tests/llms/test_google_client.py:68: TypeError
